config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once you give society priority too much power, it can easily be used to excuse abuse, censorship, or unfair treatment of minorities. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting each person’s basic rights first, then balancing those rights with the common good when there is a real conflict. From my perspective, collective interests are important, but they should not override human dignity too easily.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah mayoritas menindas minoritas atau pemerintah melampaui batas. Saya setuju bahwa ada situasi darurat, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, di mana pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sangat sempit, transparan, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, kalimat itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dasar orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the public interest” can become a cover for arbitrary power, corruption, or silencing people who are already weak. Society does matter, but it is not healthier when individuals are treated as disposable, because a community that ignores dignity and basic rights eventually becomes unjust and unstable. I do think there are limited cases, like disaster response or public health, where some individual freedom can be restricted, but that has to be narrow, necessary, and accountable. So for me, the priority should be human rights first, with social interests balanced around them, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena dari sudut pandang Amerika, memberi prioritas pada “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Dalam pengalaman politik kami, mayoritas atau pemerintah sering mengklaim bertindak demi kebaikan bersama sambil menginjak hak minoritas, jadi hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sekadar pertimbangan kedua. Saya setuju ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan terbatas, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, transparan, dan bisa diuji. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how phrases like “for the common good” can be used to excuse corruption, silence criticism, or pressure weaker people into accepting unfair treatment. That is why I believe rights must come first, and any restriction for public interest should be narrow, necessary, and accountable. Society is stronger when it protects human dignity, not when it treats individuals as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena dari perspektif Amerika, memberi prioritas umum pada kepentingan masyarakat terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sekadar catatan kaki, karena mayoritas dan pemerintah bisa salah dan menekan minoritas atas nama kebaikan bersama. Saya memang menerima bahwa dalam keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, pembatasan terbatas bisa sah, tetapi harus sempit, transparan, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dengan melindungi kebebasan dan martabat tiap orang terlebih dahulu, bukan dengan mengorbankannya demi slogan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
